markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: **GTA VI's confirmed November 19, 2026 console launch—following multiple delays from 2025 and an earlier 2026 target—has created an extended window that shapes trader positioning on the multi-outcome Polymarket.** Rockstar and Take-Two have signaled that marketing, including a potential third trailer and pre-order opening, will ramp up around summer 2026, but the late-year release leaves ample time for unrelated developments such as geopolitical shifts, music drops, or cultural milestones to resolve first. Markets currently price high-probability outcomes like a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire or specific album releases near 100% implied probability, reflecting both the long timeframe and verifiable timelines for those events, while lower-odds entries capture more speculative or distant possibilities. Traders monitor Take-Two's earnings updates and Rockstar's official communications for any further schedule changes that could compress or extend the window, alongside real-world news cycles that directly impact the listed outcomes. Historical Rockstar delays underscore the difficulty of precise timing, encouraging focus on catalysts with independent, verifiable resolution dates before the game ships.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**High trader consensus on "No Prison Time" (81.2%) stems primarily from Weinstein’s active appeals, repeated mistrials, and his advanced age and health issues, which together create substantial uncertainty around any final, enforceable sentence length.** 

The 74-year-old remains incarcerated since 2020 while litigating a 2022 California conviction carrying 16 years (under appeal) and a 2025 New York conviction on one criminal sexual act count, with sentencing pending. A third Manhattan trial on the remaining rape allegation ended in a hung-jury mistrial in mid-May 2026—the second such outcome in roughly a year—highlighting persistent prosecutorial challenges in securing unanimous verdicts on the most serious charges. 

Plea discussions reported earlier in 2026 and Weinstein’s documented medical conditions further support market-implied odds that appeals or negotiations could result in time served or minimal additional incarceration rather than multi-decade consecutive terms. Lower-probability ranges (under 8% each) reflect the baseline risk of upheld convictions producing longer sentences, but the dominant narrative centers on legal volatility and practical barriers to extended prison time.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: France leads the 2026 World Cup winner market at 18.1% implied probability, narrowly ahead of Spain, with England, Argentina, and Portugal clustered immediately behind. The tight distribution reflects a 48-team field with substantial parity among elite squads, where injuries, fixture congestion, and knockout variance can quickly shift momentum among the top six contenders.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market on the strength of his California governorship, national fundraising reach, and consistent visibility on policy issues like climate and abortion. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws support from progressive voters through her House record and media presence, while Jon Ossoff and Kamala Harris reflect Senate experience and prior national exposure. Josh Shapiro and Pete Buttigieg sit lower amid competition from other governors and former cabinet officials. In this wide-open field, name recognition, donor networks, ideological positioning, and performance in early primary states serve as primary differentiators. Trader consensus could shift with upcoming midterm results, primary debates, or endorsements that help consolidate support behind one contender.
